#  We’re always looking for talented, motivated and excellent people to join our team 
Send your CV 
![](https://parityqc.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/Careers-Header-Image2.png)
Quantum computers are transitioning from academic experiments into industry-ready hardware and will soon be solving real-world problems which are currently impossible to fathom. With its flourishing ecosystem, quantum computing is one of the most promising and exciting sectors in which to start a career.
At ParityQC we are at the forefront of this growing industry. As the first quantum architecture company, we design the blueprints and software for solving optimization problems on quantum computers. We are lucky enough to collaborate with world-leading organizations, making ground-breaking discoveries together. We are looking for talented people to join us in our journey in shaping the future of quantum computing. If you think you’d be an awesome fit in our team, have a look at our open positions below, or send your CV and cover letter to recruiting@parityqc.com
## Director of Operations Germany
As we continue to scale internationally and expand our research and partner network, we are looking for a Director of Operations in Germany to build on the operational foundation of our Hamburg site (onsite or based in Innsbruck).
In this role, you will play an important role in our transformation from a research-led to a customer-centric company shaped by emerging market demands — aligning our organization, financial framework, and funding strategies to foster sustainable growth, innovation, and enhanced value for our customers.
Working closely with the executive team, research leads, and external partners, you will oversee, adopt and further develop scalable operations across finance, grant management, and governance.
Key Responsibilities 
  * Support the transition from a research-focused to a customer-centric organization, fostering a culture that anticipates and responds to evolving market needs
  * Ensure that company structures, funding models, and financial processes are fully aligned to drive sustainable growth and operational excellence
  * Lead budgeting, forecasting, and long-term financial planning
  * Ensure financial discipline and adherence to budgetary constraints
  * Guarantee funding compliance and audit readiness across all publicly funded projects
  * Establish transparent reporting standards for executives, partners, and funding bodies
  * Implement governance and risk management frameworks aligned with national regulations and international growth
  * Strengthen cross-site collaboration between Hamburg and Innsbruck
  * Translate technical developments into structured operational processes
  * Lead negotiations within grant consortia and public tender frameworks


**This is a high-trust leadership role with significant ownership.**
Skills 
**Required**
  * Proven leadership experience in operations, finance, or general management in a high-growth, technology-driven environment
  * Expertise in aligning organizational structures, funding processes, and financial frameworks with business strategy.
  * Ability to support a company-wide shift from research-driven to customer-centric operations
  * Experience with EU and national funding frameworks and grant administration


  * Solid background in budgeting, financial planning, and financial controlling.


  * Ability to operate effectively across multiple locations and jurisdictions
  * Confident negotiator with excellent stakeholder management skills
  * Structured, analytical thinker with strong execution capabilities
  * Comfortable operating in complex, interdisciplinary environments
  * Fluency in English and German


**Preferred**
  * Interest in technical topics – high motivation to understand ParityQC’s technology on a high level
  * Tried and tested experience in the B2B technology ecosystem


  * Strong communication skills across technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  * Experience in deep-tech, hardware, or research-intensive environments is considered an advantage.
  * Familiarity with public-private funding ecosystems is a plus.


What we offer 
  * A high-impact leadership role at the core of a pioneering quantum architecture company.
  * Direct collaboration with founders and executive leadership, with real influence on strategic direction and organizational design.
  * The opportunity to build and shape the operational backbone of an internationally scaling deep-tech company from the ground up.
  * Ownership of funding architecture and financial structure in a research-intensive environment with international visibility.
  * A high-trust culture with short decision paths and intellectual depth.
  * Cross-site collaboration between Hamburg and Innsbruck within a highly specialized scientific ecosystem.
  * Long-term perspective in a company defining a new computational paradigm.
  * Competitive compensation aligned with responsibility and experience, including performance-oriented components.
  * Flexible work location between Hamburg and Innsbruck and a strong commitment to professional growth.


Hard Facts 
**Please submit a copy of your CV and a cover letter stating why you would be interested in working at ParityQC and why your experience is relevant**
Scope: full-time position
Place: Innsbruck/Hamburg (Onsite & Hybrid)
Contact: recruiting@parityqc.com
In accordance with the Equal Treatment Act, we would like to point out that the minimum salary for this position in Austria is based on the IT Collective Agreement, classification LT. A market-competitive overpayment is intended and depends on your qualifications and relevant professional experience. An equivalent approach is also foreseen for Germany.
## Quantum Algorithm Developer - Fault Tolerance
We are looking for a Quantum Algorithm Developer who will join our diverse team of experts creating next generation quantum computers. The role involves mainly the development of novel fault-tolerant algorithms whose implementation will be leveraged by the ParityQC Architecture.
Your tasks 
As a Quantum Algorithm Developer, you’ll design and implement pioneering fault-tolerant quantum algorithms co-developed with our Quantum Error Correction team, shaping the foundation of the ParityQC Architecture.
These novel quantum algorithms are specifically co-designed to run on (early-)fault tolerant hardware using the parity architecture. You will utilize the specific advantages of different hardware platforms, to shape algorithms in close cooperation with our Quantum Error Correction experts. You will conduct detailed quantum resource analysis for the new algorithms and help to shape the path towards quantum advantage.
We’re looking for a creative mind with a deep understanding of fault-tolerant algorithms, ready to unlock the next level of the ParityQC Architecture.
Skills & Experience 
Required Skills & Experience
  * PhD or Master’s degree in (theoretical) physics, mathematics or related subject with a focus on quantum computation
  * Deep understanding of fault tolerant quantum algorithms
  * Programming skills, in particular Python
  * High degree of creativity and pro-activeness
  * Ability to work self-initiated and independently, experience in research
  * Fluency in English


Preferred Experience
  * Quantum error correction codes and/or stabilizer codes
  * A proven track record in quantum algorithms development
  * Experience in classical algorithm development and complexity analysis
  * German skills are a plus, but not required


What we offer 
We are a pioneering spin-off shaping the future of quantum computing. There are no established paths in what we do — we invent, explore, and define the standards for a new era of computation.
We offer the freedom to conduct and publish your research in peer-reviewed journals, collaborate within an international and motivated team, and work flexibly — including remote options.
Hard facts 
  * **Scope** : Full-time position
  * **Place** : Innsbruck/Hamburg
  * **Contact** : recruiting@parityqc.com


_In accordance with the Equal Treatment Act, we would like to point out that the minimum salary for this position in Austria is based on the IT Collective Agreement, classification ST1. A market-competitive overpayment is intended and depends on your qualifications and relevant professional experience. An equivalent approach is also foreseen for Germany._

## Quantum Algorithm Developer – Hamiltonian Simulation
We are looking for a Quantum Algorithm Developer specialized in Hamiltonian simulations who will join our algorithms and use case team.
Your Tasks 
As a Quantum Algorithm Developer for Hamiltonian simulations, you will explore classically intractable dynamical processes in physical systems. Quantum computers open a path to address these challenges, and you will design novel Hamiltonian-simulation algorithms across all layers of the quantum computing stack. By leveraging and advancing the ParityQC Architecture, you will help move quantum computing toward practical, near-term and early fault-tolerant applications.
You will join a collaborative research team where strong physics expertise matters more than prior quantum-computing experience. If you bring deep physical intuition and curiosity, we will give you the space and support to grow into the quantum-algorithm domain.
Skills & Experience 
  * PhD in quantum physics or theoretical physics
  * Strong background in one or more of the following:


– Nonequilibrium quantum many-body physics  
– Quantum statistical mechanics  
– Quantum field theory or lattice models  
– Numerical methods for quantum systems
  * Ability to translate physical models into Hamiltonian formulations
  * Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  * High degree of creativity and scientific curiosity
  * Ability to work independently and proactively
  * Fluency in English


**Preferred Experience**
  * Proven experience in modelling quantum systems or quantum information processes
  * Familiarity with quantum-simulation frameworks (e.g., QuTiP, Qiskit, PennyLane)
  * Exposure to quantum algorithms or quantum-computing concepts (e.g., Trotterisation, variational methods)
  * Experience implementing and validating numerical simulations of quantum dynamics


Hard Facts 
  * **Scope** : Full-time position
  * **Place** : Innsbruck/Hamburg
  * **Contact** : recruiting@parityqc.com

## Finance Manager
As our company continues to grow rapidly, we are expanding our finance team to meet increasing business demands and provide both operational and strategic support. We are seeking a Finance Manager, who will contribute to operational finance tasks, support strategic initiatives, and help integrate future subsidiaries into our financial framework.
This role is ideal for someone who combines solid knowledge of accounting and financial operations with first experience in strategic financial planning and project management. You will play a key role in financial reporting, controlling, and supporting the leadership team in financial decision-making. You’ll join a team that thrives on collaboration, values ownership over hierarchy, and seeks meaningful impact — in finance and beyond.
Key Responsibilities 
  * **Financial Planning & Analysis**: Support budgeting, forecasting, and financial modeling processes, including the integration of new subsidiaries and business units.
  * **Accounting & Operations**: Ensure accurate and timely booking of financial transactions; coordinate with external accountants and auditors as needed.
  * **Strategic Project Support** : Contribute to strategic initiatives, support financial aspects of internal projects, and provide decision-making support with financial insights.
  * **Controlling & Monitoring:** Support in tracking KPIs, variances, and cash flows, and assist in preparing internal reports to ensure financial transparency across teams.
  * **Process Optimization** : Identify and implement improvements in financial processes and tools, working closely with tech, operations, and leadership.
  * **Cross-Functional Collaboration** : Interface with operations (Grant, Legal, HR) and external partners to ensure financial alignment across departments.


Skills & Experience 
**Required Skills & Experience**
  * **Educational Background** : Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Economics or a comparable field (Bachelor, Master)
  * **Professional Experience** : 2–4 years of relevant experience in a finance role (e.g. controlling, FP&A, accounting); strategic exposure (e.g. budgeting, project finance) is a plus.
  * **Accounting Know-How** : Ability to handle operational accounting topics and communicate confidently with tax advisors and external accountants.
  * **Analytical & Strategic Thinking**: Ability to understand the bigger picture while working with financial detail; comfortable with numbers and strategic context alike.
  * **Clarity & Precision**: In a fast-moving and complex environment, clarity is advantage. We value precision in communication, decision-making, and execution.
  * **Proactivity & Ownership**: Hands-on attitude with a structured and reliable way of working.
  * **Language Skills** : German & English C1/C2


**Preferred Skills & Experience**
  * **Regulatory Environment** : Expertise in different regulatory environments (Germany, Austria & further)
  * **Tool Competence** : Familiarity with financial software tools, dashboards, and Excel-based modelling.
  * **Project Exposure** : First experience in interdisciplinary projects or strategic initiatives with developmental or out of the box character.


What we offer 
  * **Strategic Impact & Variety**: Operate across accounting, controlling, and financial planning in a highly interdisciplinary team.
  * **Autonomy & Responsibility**: Step into a role with broad responsibilities, where your expertise and judgment are trusted and valued.
  * **Collaborative Culture** : Join a company culture built on respect, curiosity, and teamwork.
  * **Growth & Learning**: Develop your skills and gain exposure to strategic financial management.
  * **Flexibility & Work-Life Balance**: Benefit from flexible working hours, hybrid work options.
  * **Attractive Compensation** : Receive a competitive salary tailored to your experience and profile .


Hard facts 
  * **Scope** : Full-time position
  * **Place** : Innsbruck
  * **Contact** : _recruiting@parityqc.com_


_This position is subject to the collective agreement for employees in Information and Consulting (KV IT). The minimum salary is based on job group ST1 according to the applicable version of the agreement. An overpayment is foreseen depending on qualifications and relevant industry experience._

## Quantum Compiler Developer
We are seeking a Quantum Compiler Developer to join our world-class team of quantum engineers and physicists in creating next-generation quantum computing solutions. In this role, you will focus on developing and implementing algorithms that compile complex optimization problems into circuits for diverse quantum hardware platforms. Using the ParityQC Architecture, you will work on a core component of our software product, ParityOS. The compilation process involves transforming optimization problems, such as integer linear programs, into circuits with specific patterns designed for quantum chips. This role offers the opportunity to tackle computationally challenging problems, requiring innovative solutions that combine concepts like linear algebra, randomized search algorithms, machine learning, and heuristics.
Your tasks 
  * Design and implement pioneering algorithms for compiling optimization problems into quantum circuits.
  * Develop efficient, maintainable solutions in a fresh codebase, leveraging modern software engineering practices.
  * Solve complex mathematical and algorithmic puzzles at the intersection of quantum computing and classical optimization.
  * Collaborate with a highly skilled team of quantum computing experts to design a fully-featured and scalable software product.


Skills & Experience 
**Required Skills & Experience:**
  * PhD or MSc in a quantitative field (Computer Science, Physics, Mathematics, or related).
  * Strong programming skills in Python.
  * Solid understanding of algorithms and data structures.
  * Knowledge in linear algebra, graph theory, and machine learning.
  * High degree of creativity, problem-solving ability, and proactive mindset.
  * Self-motivation and ability to work independently.
  * Fluency in English, both written and spoken.


**Preferred Skills & Experience:**
  * Familiarity with software design principles (e.g., design patterns, SOLID).
  * Programming skills in Rust.
  * Experience with agile methodology and test-driven development.
  * Knowledge of quantum computing, quantum information, or quantum error correction (a plus, but not required).
  * German language skills (a plus but not required).


What we offer 
Join us as part of a pioneering spin-off company operating at the forefront of quantum computing. With no predefined templates or long-established conventions, we are inventing the future of computing from scratch, shaping a new generation of quantum hardware and software.
  * Opportunity to conduct research and publish in peer-reviewed journals.
  * Excellent work-life balance with flexible hours and home office options.
  * Innovative and international working environment with a motivated team, flat hierarchies, and a friendly atmosphere.
  * Continuous training and opportunities for personal and professional development.


Hard facts 
  * **Scope** : Full-time position
  * **Place** : Innsbruck
  * **Contact** : _recruiting@parityqc.com_


_This position is subject to the collective agreement for employees in Information and Consulting (KV IT). The minimum salary is based on job group ST1 according to the applicable version of the agreement. An overpayment is foreseen depending on qualifications and relevant industry experience._

## Quantum Error Correction Engineer
We are looking for a Quantum Error Correction Engineer who will join our team of Quantum Physicists creating blueprints and strategies for next generation quantum computers, with a specific focus on fully fault-tolerant implementations using the ParityQC Architecture.
Your tasks 
As a Quantum Error Correction Engineer, you will develop pioneering algorithms in the realm of quantum computing. These novel quantum algorithms are specifically co-designed to run on various quantum computing platforms. You will utilize the specific advantages of different quantum error correction codes or noise-biased hardware platforms to develop fully fault-tolerant strategies for implementing universal quantum algorithms in the ParityQC Architecture. You will also be involved in noise simulations to study and improve the resulting error rates of the developed implementations and find suitable near-term applications.
Skills & Experience 
**Required Skills & Experience**
  * PhD in (theoretical) physics, mathematics, or related subject with a focus on quantum computation
  * Deep understanding of quantum computation and quantum information, in particular experience with quantum error correction codes and stabilizer formalism
  * Basic programming skills
  * High degree of creativity and pro-activeness
  * Ability to work self-initiated and independently, experience in research
  * Fluency in English


**Preferred Experience**
  * A proven track record in quantum computing with a focus on implementing quantum algorithms in a fault-tolerant manner
  * Experience with object-oriented programming and software development, in particular in Python
  * Knowledge of noise-biased hardware platforms, e.g. bosonic codes


What we offer 
We are a spin-off working in one of the most exciting emerging technology fields. There are no long-time proven concepts on what we are doing – we need to invent everything from scratch, and we are therefore able to shape a whole new generation of computing. We are pioneers in this field and develop the blueprints for the upcoming generations. The position offers the possibility to conduct research and publish in peer-reviewed journals. Furthermore, we offer an appealing work environment with an international, motivated team and the flexibility to partially work from home.
**Why ParityQC?**
  * Excellent work-life balance
  * Flexible working hours with home office options
  * An innovative and international working environment
  * Motivated team, friendly atmosphere, flat hierarchies
  * Continuous training and encouragement of personal development


**Hard Facts**
  * Job: Full time
  * Remuneration based on experience
  * Place: Innsbruck or Hamburg

## Quantum Hardware Physicist
We are looking for a Quantum Hardware Physicist who will join our team of quantum scientists creating next-generation quantum computers. We partner with multiple hardware manufacturers and tailor the quantum algorithm to the specific hardware. The role is principally focused on the theoretical modelling and simulation of superconducting circuits, trapped ions, atoms, spin qubits or NV-centers based QC. Your focus will be either on one or multiple of these hardware platforms. You will develop pioneering realizations of multi-qubit gates including noise effects and support the co-designing of algorithms and error correction protocols specifically adapted to these hardware platforms, using the ParityQC Architecture. 
Your Tasks 
As a Quantum Hardware Physicist, you will theoretically model the underlying physics of superconducting circuits, trapped ions, atoms, spin qubits or NV-center hardware platforms including noise effects. You will utilize the specific advantages of superconducting circuits, trapped ions, neutral atoms, spin qubits or NV-center based quantum computers to co-design performant algorithms and to propose the experimental realization of improved qubit gates. This co-development of hardware and software requires both a profound theoretical knowledge of quantum information theory as well as a deep understanding of the specific qubit platforms. Your tasks involve creating innovative techniques and strategies to tackle optimization problems in the ParityQC Architecture for these hardware platforms.
Skills & Experience 
**Required Skills & Experience**
  * PhD degree in physics or related subject
  * Deep understanding of one of these hardware platforms: neutral atoms (Rydberg atoms), superconducting circuits, spin qubits or trapped ions
  * Deep understanding of quantum computation and quantum information
  * Programming skills, in particular Python
  * High degree of creativity and proactiveness
  * Ability to work in a self-initiated and independent way
  * Experience in research
  * Fluency in English


**Preferred Experience**
  * German skills are a plus, but not required


What we offer 
We are a spin-off working in one of the most exciting emerging technology fields. There are no long-time proven concepts on what we are doing – we need to invent everything from scratch, and we are therefore able to shape a whole new generation of quantum computers. We are pioneers in this field and develop the blueprints for the upcoming generations. The position will allow you to conduct research and publish in peer-reviewed journals.

## Software Engineer for Quantum Computing
We are looking for a Software Engineer for Quantum Computing joining our diverse team of Quantum Physicists and Quantum Software Engineers in the Use-Case-Department. In this role you will contribute to the development of our ParityQC use-case software stack, which is a unique framework for developing and testing quantum algorithms for noisy quantum hardware. You will gain insights into the whole quantum computing stack from high-level software all the way down to hardware-specific instructions. When integrating different quantum methods into our main product, ParityOS, you will be faced with challenging and exciting coding tasks, connecting quantum algorithms with classical software.
Your tasks 
As a Software Engineer for Quantum Computing, you will contribute to the development of a pioneer software product for quantum algorithm development. You will work on a rapidly growing code base, integrating different tools for quantum algorithm development into a common framework. You will implement quantum algorithms as well as the necessary tooling for the development of quantum algorithms from scratch, thereby ensuring maintainability, performance, and usability of the software stack. You will encounter challenges on both sides, quantum and classical, offering you the unique chance to grow into your role and to gain deep insights into quantum computing when solving problems as part of our world-class international team of experts.
Skills & Experience 
**Required Experience**
  * Master’s degree in informatics, Computer Science or similar
  * At least 2 years of experience in professional software development
  * Excellent programming skills, especially Python
  * High degree of curiosity and a willingness to tackle problems which are out of your comfort zone
